message:
feat(ffs-core, ffs-cli): reorder journal commit path and add btrfs superblock recovery

ffs-core: reorder the journaled commit path to perform MVCC conflict
detection BEFORE writing to the JBD2 journal. Previously, journal writes
happened first, meaning a subsequent MVCC conflict abort would leave
committed data in the journal that crash recovery would replay, corrupting
filesystem state. Now MVCC commit runs first (in-memory only), and if it
succeeds, the journal write follows. If the journal write fails after MVCC
commit, the process panics to prevent memory/disk state divergence.

Thread PersistCtx through all ext4 allocation call sites (create_file,
create_dir, write, unlink, rmdir, symlink, fallocate, truncate) so block
and inode allocation changes are written through to on-disk group
descriptors immediately, keeping the allocator and disk state consistent.

ffs-cli: add btrfs primary-superblock bootstrap recovery to fsck and
repair paths. When initial filesystem detection fails and writes are
permitted, the CLI now reads btrfs backup superblock mirrors at known
offsets (64KB at 64MB, 256GB, and 256TB), validates their checksums, and
restores the primary superblock from the best validated backup. The
detection phase then retries. Add crc32c dependency for btrfs superblock
checksum validation. Expand CLI with structured bootstrap recovery
reporting in fsck/repair JSON output.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
